steps.title: Compare the two predictions
steps.content: Political scientists expected campaign ads to reduce knowledge gaps by giving voters clear information. Cognitive scientists expected prior knowledge to make new information easier to learn, so high-knowledge participants could benefit more.

steps.title: Match the graph's effect
steps.content:
• Choice A: Says the result supports political scientists more, but benefiting high-knowledge participants points the other way.
  • Choice B: Captures an effect contrary to the political-science prediction and consistent with the cognitive-science mechanism.
  • Choice C: Denies the cognitive phenomenon even though it explains the pattern.
  • Choice D: Says the political-science prediction occurred, which conflicts with the gap not shrinking.
The answer is B.
